data.content: The project uses **MSTest v3.8.0** with the Microsoft Testing Platform (`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport=true`). Tests are categorized via `[TestCategory]`. Let me run just the Integration tests:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run integration tests with dotnet test

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run integration tests with dotnet test

data.reasoningText:
The project uses **MSTest** (v3.8.0) with the Microsoft Testing Platform. The `Directory.Build.props` has `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` set to true, which means it uses the newer Microsoft Testing Platform runner (not the older VSTest runner).

The tests have `TestCategory` attributes - "Unit" and "Integration". The user wants to run only the integration tests.

With MSTest on the Microsoft Testing Platform, to filter by category, I can use `--filter "TestCategory=Integration"` with `dotnet test`.

arguments.description: Run integration tests with dotnet test

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-4d9df0da6dfe43e6ae96c5c63ba314de && dotnet test --filter \"TestCategory=Integration\" 2>&1", "description": "Run integration tests with dotnet test", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run integration tests with dotnet test
